example: After weeks of thought, she finally made a decision about the job offer.
The students are making steady progress in their writing.

back: to try hard to do something
example: He made a real effort to speak more fluently.
It is normal to make mistakes when learning a language.
Small daily habits can make a big difference over time.

back: to study a subject in detail
example: She did extensive research before writing her essay.

back: to try as hard as you can
example: Just do your best and stay calm during the exam.
Too much screen time can do harm to young children.
The company does business with clients across Asia.

back: to clean and maintain a home
example: They share doing the housework equally.

back: to accept that you are accountable
example: A good leader takes responsibility for the team’s mistakes.

back: to use an opportunity well (or unfairly)
example: You should take advantage of the free practice tests.
Thousands took part in the climate march.

back: to do something that might fail
example: Entrepreneurs often take risks to grow their business.
We must take the costs into account before deciding.
Social media has a huge impact on young people.

back: to be able to use or reach something
example: Not everyone has access to clean drinking water.
We had a long conversation about our future plans.
The government should give priority to public health.

back: to mention something to illustrate a point
example: Can you give an example to support your argument?
Pay close attention to the examiner’s instructions.

back: to say something nice to someone
example: She paid him a compliment on his presentation.

back: to do what you said you would
example: He always keeps his promises to his children.
We promised to keep in touch after graduation.
It is hard to break the habit of checking your phone.
Driving without a licence means breaking the law.
The bright poster immediately caught my attention.

back: to make a final decision or judgement
example: After the debate, they reached a sensible conclusion.
Heavy rain caused flooding across the region.
She made a strong argument for renewable energy.
Traffic congestion is a major problem in big cities.
Education plays a key role in reducing poverty.
Tourism has a significant impact on the local economy.
It is a common misconception that talent cannot be learned.
A balanced diet is essential for good health.

back: a lot of things to do
example: Despite a busy schedule, she still finds time to read.

back: a lively, energetic way of life
example: The city is famous for its vibrant culture and nightlife.
Recycling is a viable solution to the waste problem.
They renovated the flat on a tight budget.
There was a steep increase in house prices last year.
The chart shows a gradual decline in unemployment.
There is fierce competition for places at top universities.
The internship offers ample opportunity to learn.
It is highly likely that prices will rise again.
Scientists are deeply concerned about rising sea levels.
It is widely accepted that exercise improves mood.

back: to be very sure of an opinion
example: I strongly believe that education should be free.
City life is completely different from village life.
It is perfectly normal to feel nervous before an exam.
The winter mornings here are bitterly cold.
She was fully aware of the risks involved.
The accusation was utterly ridiculous.
Online learning has become increasingly popular.

back: to be affected or decided by
example: Your success depends on consistent practice.
Poor planning can result in serious delays.
A lack of sleep can lead to health problems.

back: to help cause or improve something
example: Cars contribute to air pollution in cities.
Many students struggle to cope with exam stress.
We rely on technology more than ever before.
In this essay I will focus on two main causes.
Children benefit from spending time outdoors.
The course consists of four modules.

back: to obey a rule or request
example: All staff must comply with safety regulations.
She worked late to meet the deadline.
Volunteering is a great way to gain experience.
He decided to pursue a career in medicine.

back: to be present at a meeting
example: I have to attend a meeting this afternoon.

back: to decide on something to achieve
example: Setting clear goals keeps you motivated.

back: to succeed in reaching an aim
example: With hard work, you can achieve your goals.
Reading widely helps you develop language skills.
You must submit your application by Friday.
Parents play a vital role in a child’s education.

back: to make people more conscious of an issue
example: The campaign aims to raise awareness of recycling.
The report addresses the issue of food waste.
Climate change poses a serious threat to coastal cities.
Schools should meet the needs of every student.

back: to form a judgement from evidence
example: From the data, we can draw a clear conclusion.

back: to reduce a difference between two things
example: Technology can help bridge the gap between rich and poor.

back: to find a fair middle point
example: Students must strike a balance between study and rest.

back: to make an effort to solve something
example: Governments must tackle the problem of unemployment.
The internet has had a profound effect on society.
Cooking in batches saves a lot of time.
Buying things you never use is wasting money.
I like to spend time with my family at weekends.

back: to use up all of something
example: We have run out of time, so let’s conclude.

back: to have just enough money to live
example: Many families struggle to make ends meet.
The company cut costs by going paperless.

back: to have a break from work
example: She took some time off to recover.

back: to be unaware of how much time has passed
example: I was so absorbed that I lost track of time.
